category|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} [[JA:内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11394400}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) entrance.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} [[JA:内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11394400}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) entrance.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} [[JA:内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11394400}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) entrance.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡市の建築物]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} [[JA:内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11394400}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) entrance.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:八幡市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} [[JA:内神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11394400}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Uchi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) entrance.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]

{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]

Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi
{{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two small seats)
former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uchi Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Uchi Shrine''' (うちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchisato Uchi, {{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi (やましろのうちのおみ) : Also written "Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi." ; {{ill|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|Q18225727|WD=Q18225727|lt=Umashiuchi-no-Sukune|1=Umashiuti-no-Sukune}} (うましうちのすくね) : Also written as "Amamiuchi-no-Sukune" in documents. He is the half-brother of {{ill|lt=Takeuchi no Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} and ancestor of the Yamashiro Uchi no Omi. He is enshrined in the co-seat. === History === Shrine tradition says the shrine was founded after the death of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi. Umashiuchi-no-Sukune, enshrined in the co-seat, is thought to have been later merged as ancestral deity of Yamashiro no Uchi no OmiSee Yawata Tourism Association page in external links.. Details of Yamashiro no Uchi no Omi are unclear; it may be a clan name rather than personal name. In the {{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} there is an entry for a clan "Uchi no Omi" descended from Prince {{ill|lt=Hikofutoni no Makoto no Mikoto|Q11487911|WD=Q11487911|1=Hikofutsuoshi no Makoto no Mikoto}}, son of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōgen|Q333572|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Engi|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, as "two seats of Uchi Shrine," and is ranked as shikinaisha. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, it was called "Kasuga Munakata Shrine." According to the {{ill|lt=Jinja Meisaichō|Q11590704|WD=Q11590704|1=jinjameisaichou}}, it suffered war damage in the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} during Daiei-era conflicts and was moved to the current location in the Tenshō era. The old site was southeast of the present shrine and remains in the place name "Uchisato Furumiya." From Oct 2001 to Sep 2002, current honden and precinct were improved as the "Uchisato district construction project." === Precincts === [[File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] The precinct is at the west edge of the Uchisato settlement, surrounded by rice fields. The honden is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roof, built in 2002. An old honden stands to the left of the current honden. Like the present honden, it is one-bay nagare-zukuri with copper roof. During the 2001-2002 works it was relocated and preserved from the current honden site. At that time an ink inscription dated {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}) was found, helping identify its construction period. The building is registered as a Kyoto Prefecture cultural property. File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Uchi-jinja (Yawata) honden (old).jpg|Old honden (Kyoto Prefecture registered cultural property) === Subsidiary shrines === * Itsukushima Shrine * Kushi Shrine * Inari Shrine === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Property === * Old honden (architecture) - registered in 1983. === Kyoto Prefecture Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === * Uchi Shrine Cultural Property Environmental Conservation District === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yawata|Q260923|WD=Q260923|1=Yawata}}Uchisato Uchi 1 *: The place name "Uchisato" derives from this shrine. It is an old place name recorded as "Uchi-gō" in Tsuzuki District, Yamashiro, in the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}. '''Access''' * From {{ill|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|Q1188274|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan|1=Keihan Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Keihan Main Line|Q2598752|WD=Q2598752|1=Keihan Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu-Hachimangū Station|Q1977185|WD=Q1977185|1=Iwashimizu-hachimangū Station}}, take {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} and get off at "Uchisato" bus stop (about 5 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * ''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Uchi Shrine === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Uchi Shrine|Q88535800|WD=Q88535800|1=Uchi Shrine (Yamato)}} - shikinaisha of Uchi District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}. As with this shrine, a close relation with Uchi no Omi is indicated. === External links === {{Commons|Category:Uchi-jinja (Yawata)}} *[http://www.kankou-yawata.org/htdocs/?page_id=16#j05 Uchi Shrine] (Yawata Tourism Association website) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uchi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yawata, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]]
